en: James, a servant of God and of the Lord Jesus Christ, To the twelve tribes in the Dispersion: Greetings.
zh: 神和主耶穌基督的僕人雅各，向散居各地的十二支派問安。

en: Count it all joy, my brothers, when you meet trials of various kinds,
zh: 我的弟兄們，你們遭遇各種試煉的時候，都要看為喜樂；

en: for you know that the testing of your faith produces steadfastness.
zh: 因為知道你們的信心經過考驗，就產生忍耐。

en: And let steadfastness have its full effect, that you may be perfect and complete, lacking in nothing.
zh: 但忍耐要堅持到底 ，使你們可以完全，毫無缺乏。

en: If any of you lacks wisdom, let him ask God, who gives generously to all without reproach, and it will be given him.
zh: 你們中間若有人缺少智慧，就當向那厚賜眾人，而且不斥責人的 神祈求，他就必得著。

en: But let him ask in faith, with no doubting, for the one who doubts is like a wave of the sea that is driven and tossed by the wind.
zh: 可是，他應該憑著信心祈求，不要有疑惑；因為疑惑的人，就像被風吹蕩翻騰的海浪。

en: For that person must not suppose that he will receive anything from the Lord;
zh: 那樣的人，不要想從主得到甚麼；

en: he is a double-minded man, unstable in all his ways.
zh: 因為三心兩意的人，在他的一切道路上，都搖擺不定。

en: Let the lowly brother boast in his exaltation,
zh: 卑微的弟兄應當以高升為榮；

en: and the rich in his humiliation, because like a flower of the grass he will pass away.
zh: 富足的也不應該以降卑為辱；因為他如同草上的花，必要過去。

en: For the sun rises with its scorching heat and withers the grass; its flower falls, and its beauty perishes. So also will the rich man fade away in the midst of his pursuits.
zh: 太陽一出，熱風一吹，草必枯乾，花必凋謝，它的美容就消失了；富足的人也必在他的奔波經營中這樣衰落。

en: Blessed is the man who remains steadfast under trial, for when he has stood the test he will receive the crown of life, which God has promised to those who love him.
zh: 能忍受試煉的人，是有福的；因為他經過考驗之後，必得著生命的冠冕，這冠冕是主應許給愛他的人的。

en: Let no one say when he is tempted, "I am being tempted by God," for God cannot be tempted with evil, and he himself tempts no one.
zh: 人被試探，不可說“我被 神試探”；因為 神不能被惡試探，他也不試探任何人。

en: But each person is tempted when he is lured and enticed by his own desire.
zh: 每一個人受試探，都是被自己的私慾所勾引誘惑的。

en: Then desire when it has conceived gives birth to sin, and sin when it is fully grown brings forth death.
zh: 私慾懷了胎，就生出罪；罪長成了，就產生死亡。

en: Do not be deceived, my beloved brothers.
zh: 我親愛的弟兄們，不要看錯了。

en: Every good gift and every perfect gift is from above, coming down from the Father of lights with whom there is no variation or shadow due to change.
zh: 各樣美好的賞賜，各樣完備的恩賜，都是從上面、從眾光之父降下來的，他本身並沒有改變，也沒有轉動的影子。

en: Of his own will he brought us forth by the word of truth, that we should be a kind of firstfruits of his creation.
zh: 他憑著自己的旨意，藉著真理的道生了我們，使我們作他所造的萬物中初熟的果子。

en: Know this, my beloved brothers: let every person be quick to hear, slow to speak, slow to anger;
zh: 我親愛的弟兄們，你們要知道，人人都應該快快地聽，慢慢地說，慢一點動怒；

en: for the anger of man does not produce the righteousness that God requires.
zh: 因為人的忿怒並不能成全 神的義。

en: Therefore put away all filthiness and rampant wickedness and receive with meekness the implanted word, which is able to save your souls.
zh: 所以你們應當擺脫一切污穢和所有的邪惡，以溫柔的心領受 神栽種的道；這道能救你們的靈魂。

en: But be doers of the word, and not hearers only, deceiving yourselves.
zh: 你們應該作行道的人，不要單作聽道的人，自己欺騙自己；

en: For if anyone is a hearer of the word and not a doer, he is like a man who looks intently at his natural face in a mirror.
zh: 因為人若只作聽道的人，不作行道的人，他就像一個人對著鏡子看自己本來的面貌，

en: For he looks at himself and goes away and at once forgets what he was like.
zh: 看過走開以後，馬上就忘記自己的樣子。

en: But the one who looks into the perfect law, the law of liberty, and perseveres, being no hearer who forgets but a doer who acts, he will be blessed in his doing.
zh: 唯有詳細察看那使人自由的全備的律法，並且時常遵守的人，他不是聽了就忘記，而是實行出來，就必因自己所作的蒙福。

en: If anyone thinks he is religious and does not bridle his tongue but deceives his heart, this person's religion is worthless.
zh: 如果有人自以為虔誠，卻不約束他的舌頭，反而自己欺騙自己，這人的虔誠是沒有用的。

en: Religion that is pure and undefiled before God and the Father is this: to visit orphans and widows in their affliction, and to keep oneself unstained from the world.
zh: 在父 神看來，純潔無玷污的虔誠，就是照顧患難中的孤兒寡婦，並且保守自己不被世俗所污染。
